verdict_reasoning: FN1 is a secreted glycoprotein with no transmembrane domain, GPI anchor, or intrinsic membrane-anchoring mechanism. It reaches the extracellular face through reversible, non-covalent binding to cell-surface integrin receptors (α5β1, αVβ3), entering a cell-associated ECM fibril network. However, this integrin-mediated recruitment is transient and wash-removable—FN1 itself has no stable physical link to the plasma membrane. The soluble secreted form is the canonical biological entity; integrin binding nucleates matrix assembly but does not constitute surface accessibility of the protein body via its own mechanism. FN1 is classified as secreted-only, not as a stable surface-associated protein.
